feature_list.test_description: Verify the core directly supports Normal-mode I2C operation at 100 Kbps.
feature_list.test_constraints: Only direct support for 100 Kbps is specified; implementation-dependent availability is noted.
test_steps: Configure the core for 100 Kbps I2C operation.
Initiate an I2C transfer using SCL and SDA.
Measure the resulting bus clock rate.
expected_results: The core operates in 100 Kbps mode.
SCL/SDA signaling corresponds to approximately 100 Kbps operation.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation, where implemented.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Normal: 100Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core supports Fast-mode I2C operation at 400 Kbps when correct hardware is included.
feature_list.test_constraints: 400 Kbps support depends on correct hardware being included.
test_steps: Configure the core for 400 Kbps I2C operation.
Initiate an I2C transfer using SCL and SDA.
Measure the resulting bus clock rate.
expected_results: The core operates in 400 Kbps mode.
SCL/SDA signaling corresponds to approximately 400 Kbps operation.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation, where correct hardware is included.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Fast: 400Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant Classic interface signal set.
feature_list.test_constraints: Compliance details beyond the listed interface signals are not specified.
test_steps: Inspect the WISHBONE interface during a representative bus access.
Verify presence and use of wb_clk_i, wb_rst_i, wb_adr_i, wb_dat_i, wb_dat_o, wb_we_i, wb_stb_i, wb_cyc_i, wb_ack_o, and wb_inta_o.
expected_results: The listed WISHBONE Classic interface signals are exposed by the core.
The interface uses the specified WISHBONE signal set during access.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core shall implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.requirement_text: The core features a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.

feature_list.test_description: Verify each WISHBONE access completes in two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_constraints: Applicable to WISHBONE accesses using wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i.
test_steps: Start a WISHBONE access by asserting wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i.
Count wb_clk_i cycles until wb_ack_o is asserted.
expected_results: wb_ack_o is asserted on the second clock cycle of the access.
The access duration is two clock cycles.
feature_list.requirement_description: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset operates as active high when ARST_LVL is set to 1'b1.
test_steps: Set ARST_LVL to 1'b1.
Drive arst_i high, then inactive.
Observe reset behavior of the core.
expected_results: The core resets when arst_i is high.
The core does not remain in reset after arst_i returns inactive.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset operates as active low when ARST_LVL is set to 1'b0.
test_steps: Set ARST_LVL to 1'b0.
Drive arst_i low, then inactive.
Observe reset behavior of the core.
expected_results: The core resets when arst_i is low.
The core does not remain in reset after arst_i returns inactive.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes SCL pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external connection.
test_steps: Inspect the external SCL interface of the core.
Verify presence of scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe and exercise a representative transfer.
expected_results: The core exposes SCL input, output, and output-enable signals.
The SCL signals are used during external bus connection behavior.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external connection.
test_steps: Inspect the external SDA interface of the core.
Verify presence of sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe and exercise a representative transfer.
expected_results: The core exposes SDA input, output, and output-enable signals.
The SDA signals are used during external bus connection behavior.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable
